Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Revenue grew 12 percent year-over-year supported by enterprise demand for our cloud platform and continued adoption of productivity offerings across commercial and consumer segments.

Operating expenses rose modestly as we expanded research and development headcount to support next-generation product investments in artificial intelligence and cloud infrastructure.

Cash flow from operations remained strong during the quarter, providing flexibility for capital returns through share repurchases and dividends, as well as continued strategic investment.

We expect macroeconomic conditions to remain volatile through the fiscal year, with currency headwinds creating modest variability in reported results for our international business segments.

Our Intelligent Cloud segment delivered double-digit revenue growth driven by Azure and server products, reflecting continued customer migration from on-premises workloads to cloud-native architectures.

Item 3. Other Information
